architecture_fit.note: Threshold + morphological closing + run-length scan directly solves the measurement goal (canonical MAE 0.81, corr 0.95); goal is synthetic/measurement-only so the thin transformer-delta framing is acceptable.

baseline_comparison.note: Two strawmen under identical conditions: predict-mean clearly fails (3.84 vs 0.81), but the raw-threshold strawman is near-tied with denoised at canonical d, slightly weakening the central claim.

faithfulness.note: No model to patch; raw-vs-denoised serves as a component ablation of the closing step, but the README never proposes what a real-transformer causal check would look like.

operating_range.note: Swept across L=1..16 and d=0.3..0.9; degradation at edges is exposed (hurts at d=0.3 and short L), though span is ~1.2 orders of magnitude, not a full 2.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Entire circuit is hand-built with no training — a hand-set mechanism that reproduces the target behaviour, exactly the bonus case.

visual_judgement.note: Demo shows per-sample weights with threshold line, true-run band and detected-run coloring, plus a clean MAE-by-difficulty summary comparing denoised/raw/baseline; legible and checkable.

visualisation_rationale.note: Why-this-visualisation justifies axes, baseline, and grain, tying run-length and difficulty to the goal's exact metrics.

notes: A clean hand-built measurement circuit (threshold + morphological closing + run-length scan) that solves the synthetic longest-run task well at canonical difficulty (MAE 0.81, corr 0.95) and massively beats the predict-mean baseline. Strengths are the fully hardcoded mechanism and a clear visualisation/rationale; weaknesses are that the headline denoising step barely beats — and at d=0.3 and short runs underperforms — the raw-threshold strawman, and there is no proposed causal check mapping the circuit to a real transformer. Mean of scored items ≈4.1, landing it solidly in 'good'.
